themes: Services at Higher Levels of Health System

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.remarks: The school eye health program in the intervention areas are implemented by the Ministries of Education and Health with support from Sightsavers. Sightsavers’ school health integrated program (SHIP) objective is to improve the health, and educational outcomes of school aged children in Liberia. Working in partnership with the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Education to trains schoolteachers to deliver eye vision screening and provide deworming tablets to school aged children in the intervention areas. | No, there is no policy or regulation to limit vision screening by teachers in the school.

Operationalization Strategies.remarks:
This training manual is designed to help teachers understand and deliver integrated deworming and vision screening as part of the School Health Integrated Program (SHIP) for Liberia. The manual has an integrated approach and is intended to help teachers implement various school health interventions such as vision screening and deworming using available resources. It is not exhaustive but provides an insight for teachers to deliver simple, effective, and efficient integrated school health activities for school age children. It is expected as the Liberian educational system becomes familiar with an integrated approach to school health, additional interventions such as hearing screening, malaria education or oral health may possibly be added in similar manner.
The manual consists of two sections, the first section covers content for vision screening while the second section deals with deworming. Each section gives a background information and guidelines for the teacher to conduct vision screening and deworming accordingly using an integrated approach. The sections contain the objectives, strategies, steps and community engagement contents that will enable a teacher to engage their students in the classroom and the school community. 
In addition to the two sections, we have four appendices. Appendix one is the school register that captures the details of school age children and appendix two is the school summary sheet which will be used to summarize entries from the school register for reporting. Appendices three and four are the district and county summary forms, which are to be completed by the District Education Officers (DEOs) and the County Education Officers (CEOs) respectively. | Over 2000 teachers and support staff working within the Ministries of Education and Health have benefited from the school eye health training on how to to implement screening and deworming in schools throughout in the intervention area. | The school eye health program in the intervention areas are implemented by the Ministries of Education and Health with support from Sightsavers. Sightsavers’ school health integrated program (SHIP) objective is to improve the health, and educational outcomes of school aged children in Liberia. Working in partnership with the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Education to trains schoolteachers to deliver eye vision screening and provide deworming tablets to school aged children in the intervention areas. | The nursing curriculum for nursing training does not include a dedicated section on school health and/or eye health?

Coordination and Integration.remarks:
The school eye health program in the intervention areas are implemented by the Ministries of Education and Health with support from Sightsavers. Sightsavers’ school health integrated program (SHIP) objective is to improve the health, and educational outcomes of school aged children in Liberia. Working in partnership with the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Education to trains schoolteachers to deliver eye vision screening and provide deworming tablets to school aged children in the intervention areas. | This training manual is designed to help teachers understand and deliver integrated deworming and vision screening as part of the School Health Integrated Program (SHIP) for Liberia. The manual has an integrated approach and is intended to help teachers implement various school health interventions such as vision screening and deworming using available resources. It is not exhaustive but provides an insight for teachers to deliver simple, effective, and efficient integrated school health activities for school age children. It is expected as the Liberian educational system becomes familiar with an integrated approach to school health, additional interventions such as hearing screening, malaria education or oral health may possibly be added in similar manner.
The manual consists of two sections, the first section covers content for vision screening while the second section deals with deworming. Each section gives a background information and guidelines for the teacher to conduct vision screening and deworming accordingly using an integrated approach. The sections contain the objectives, strategies, steps and community engagement contents that will enable a teacher to engage their students in the classroom and the school community. 
In addition to the two sections, we have four appendices. Appendix one is the school register that captures the details of school age children and appendix two is the school summary sheet which will be used to summarize entries from the school register for reporting. Appendices three and four are the district and county summary forms, which are to be completed by the District Education Officers (DEOs) and the County Education Officers (CEOs) respectively.

Accessibility and Inclusivity.remarks: Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. Teachers will also be screened for refractive errors and provided with glasses according to their needs. | Medical treatment for child eye conditions are available, however, children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. | Surgical treatment facilities are available to treat children for eye conditions. Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. | Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions or who require low vision care are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.remarks: Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. Teachers will also be screened for refractive errors and provided with glasses according to their needs. | Medical treatment for child eye conditions are available, however, children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. | Surgical treatment facilities are available to treat children for eye conditions. Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. | Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions or who require low vision care are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. | Other care or cross-referral for Children are available. | The project has trained optometric technicians to add to the additional human resource and supplement the limited number of existing eye health workers in Liberia to process access to eye health counselling and advice which has strengthen the existing referral pathway to ensure children with other eye health conditions can access high quality services at designated referral centers. This includes children with pediatric cataract and other eye conditions identified during vision screening and sight testing.

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_keywords: Ministry, Government, plan, policy + delivered, included, aligned, partnership, stakeholders
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_remarks:
1.1 Legislation: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health in Liberia and is aligned with both the Liberia Education Sector Plan and the Government of Liberia’s NTD Master Plan. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: School health is included in the National Education Policy. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education  and is aligned with the Liberia Education Sector Plan. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education  and is aligned with the School Health Policy. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health in Liberia and is aligned with the Liberia Education Sector Plan, the Government of Liberia’s NTD Master Plan and Health Policy. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health in Liberia and is aligned with the Liberia Education Sector Plan, the Government of Liberia’s NTD Master Plan and Health Strategic plan. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health in Liberia and is aligned with the Liberia Education Sector Plan, the Government of Liberia’s NTD Master Plan and Eye Health Policy and Strategic Plan. | 1.2 Policies, Sector Strategies and Plans: SHIP is delivered in partnership with the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health in Liberia and is aligned with the Liberia Education Sector Plan, The school Health Policy, the Government of Liberia’s NTD Master Plan and Eye Health Policy and Strategic Plan, Nutrition, Sexual and Reproductive Health and Water, Sanitation and Hygiene for Schools. | 1.4 Key stakeholders and coordination:  Which ministry/ministries is/are 
responsible for school health and/or 
school eye health in the country?
 If multiple, is there a primary or 
lead Ministry?Ministries of Education and Health. The Ministry of Education is the primary lead. | Is there a designated unit or officer 
for school health and/or school eye 
health within the ministry structure?: The department of school health at both Ministries which is headed by a Director. At the Ministry of Education, this department is under the Student Personnel Services and at the Ministry of Health under Community Health. | 1.4 Key stakeholders and coordination:  Is there any mechanism for  coordination of school health and/
 or school eye health between the 
ministries of health and education?: The collaboration of the Eye Health Technical Working Group, which involves the Ministries of Education and Health for integrated eye health services, especially the SHIP project concept, in their respective sectoral plans, ensuring that these plans are robustly monitored in order to reduce the impact of visual ailments for the Liberian population especially in rural communities. | The National Social Protection Policy and Strategy is a 5-year national wide document developed by the Government of Liberia aimed at building a social protection system that tackles extreme poverty, vulnerability and inequality in Liberia whilst contributing to economic growth and peace and security. The Social Protection policy provides a unique opportunity to make a difference in the lives of the extremely poor and most vulnerable segments of the Liberian population, by integrating them into mainstream development. School health is all included in this social protection.

Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_remarks: Primary eye care staff are available in our intervention area have the necessary skills required to provide basic screening and detect other eye health conditions in school age children. | Primary eye care staff are available in our intervention area have the basic skill set required to provide primary screening and detect other eye health conditions in school age children. Optometry Technician, Vision Technician, Ophthalmic Nurses, Cataract Surgeon provide more specialized services at the hospitals. | The primary eye care services are linked with school health services within the intervention area. | Ophthalmic technicians provide refraction services to school age children. | Each intervention area has one secondary eye care facility that provide refractive error (myopia, hypermetropia, and astigmatism ) services in the intervention area. | Health screening activities are currently being undertaken in the community through outreach programs under the leadership of the county health teams working alongside district and community leaders. | 2.4: Community level: We have primary eye care staff available in our intervention area that provide eye health promotion and disease prevention services.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.barrier_remarks: 2.8: Data: Monitoring and supportive supervision takes place in the intervention area, but needs to be strengthened. In situations where resources for eye health are limited, decisions need to be made to ensure that the programmes not only address public health problems but are also implementing in a way that is effective, efficient and sustainable.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.enabler_remarks: 2.6: Tertiary level: The Phebe Hospital and Eye Clinic is a referral center within our intervention area. The center has a Ophthalmologists who specializes in the eye care. He performs medical and surgical eye care. This service is also link to the school eye health. Additionally, school children that need peadiatric eye care services that are not currently offered at Phebe are referred to the JFK Hospital in Monrovia, outside the intervention area. | 2.5: Secondary level: Secondary eye care staff are available in our intervention area. They have the necessary skill set required to teat and detect other eye health conditions in school age children. | Each intervention area has one secondary eye care facility that provide refractive error (myopia, hypermetropia, and astigmatism ) services in the intervention area. | 2.7: Referral pathways: Bringing the services to the people by  screening children in the schools
Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.barrier_remarks: 2.8: Data: there are no specific indicators linked to school eye health, however, data collected from school programmes is reported at the district level. Information flow from schools to health authorities from the county>districts>region>national
